body: Nature, Published online: 17 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10650-0 Biochemical, structural and cell biological analyses reveal that UAP56 (DDX39B) assembles with a TREX-2–like module that redirects non-functional polyadenylated RNAs from export to degradation.

title: Cortical development dynamics across autism spectrum disorder mouse models
summary:
Nature, Published online: 17 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10679-1

Using single-nucleus multi-omic sequencing, diverse autism spectrum disorder-linked gene mutations converge on transient, stage-specific disruptions in early…
body: Nature, Published online: 17 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10679-1 Using single-nucleus multi-omic sequencing, diverse autism spectrum disorder-linked gene mutations converge on transient, stage-specific disruptions in early brain development, and highlight sex-specific gene expression alterations.

title: A blastoporal organizer in a ctenophore
summary:
Nature, Published online: 17 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10643-z

Experiments using the comb jelly Mnemiopsis leidyi and the sea anemone Nematostella vectensis reveal that the emergence of a core signalling pathway may have…
body: Nature, Published online: 17 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10643-z Experiments using the comb jelly Mnemiopsis leidyi and the sea anemone Nematostella vectensis reveal that the emergence of a core signalling pathway may have been a key innovation enabling the transition to multicellularity in animals.

title: Confined migration induces non-lethal DNA damage in developing neurons
summary:
Nature, Published online: 17 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10648-8

The migration of neurons in developing cerebral and cerebellar cortices is accompanied by massive DNA double-strand breaks due to mechanostress during passag…
body: Nature, Published online: 17 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10648-8 The migration of neurons in developing cerebral and cerebellar cortices is accompanied by massive DNA double-strand breaks due to mechanostress during passage through narrow interstitial spaces.

title: Analysis of 173,303 exomes and genomes in the Pakistan Genome Resource
summary:

The Pakistan Genome Resource compiles biobank data from 173,303 individuals with high familial relatedness, broadening the catalogue of human genetic variati…
body: Nature, Published online: 17 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10667-5 The Pakistan Genome Resource compiles biobank data from 173,303 individuals with high familial relatedness, broadening the catalogue of human genetic variation and establishing a population-specific genomic reference for Pakistan.

title: CHPO coordinates chilling recovery and nitrogen use in rice
summary:

body: Nature, Published online: 17 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10682-6 A rice gene, CHPO, links chilling resilience with nitrogen-use efficiency, revealing a domestication-shaped regulatory mechanism that could guide breeding of climate-resilient, sustainable rice varieties.

title: Optical metasurfaces for general vision processing on the edge
summary:
Nature, Published online: 17 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10635-z

By embedding core computer vision principles into a large-scale optical metasurface, an efficient vision processing system using far fewer parameters is demo…
body: Nature, Published online: 17 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10635-z By embedding core computer vision principles into a large-scale optical metasurface, an efficient vision processing system using far fewer parameters is demonstrated to outperform many digital models and enables deployment on edge devices.

title: Cucurbituril-based anion-conducting membranes with supramolecular nanopores
summary:
Nature, Published online: 17 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10666-6

A supramolecular strategy, in which uniform, dynamic nanopores are constructed, overcomes the limitations of limited structural control, mechanical fragility…
body: Nature, Published online: 17 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10666-6 A supramolecular strategy, in which uniform, dynamic nanopores are constructed, overcomes the limitations of limited structural control, mechanical fragility or demanding synthesis in nanoporous anion-conducting membranes, providing a versatile tool for molecularly engineering transport pathways.

title: Reimagining machine vision with optical computing
summary:
Nature, Published online: 17 June 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01891-0

A general-purpose artificial-intelligence vision system for use in image-sensing devices has been developed by embedding fundamentals of core computer-vision…
body: Nature, Published online: 17 June 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01891-0 A general-purpose artificial-intelligence vision system for use in image-sensing devices has been developed by embedding fundamentals of core computer-vision operations into a light-manipulating planar material called an optical metasurface. A prototype enables accurate, real-time perception and processing across diverse tasks, suggesting that this could be a solution for rapid, low-energy, on-device vision intelligence.

body: Nature, Published online: 17 June 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01882-1 The European Union has declared that it wants to stop using animals in chemical safety testing. Its goal will need a timeline and a serious funding commitment.
